title: Hungry Hungry Homer

jokes.text: Bart and Lisa's synchronized 'Meh' response and spelling it out for Homer
jokes.setup: Homer excitedly asks kids about visiting Blockoland
jokes.punchline: Deadpan 'Meh' followed by literal spelling: 'M-E-H. Meh.'
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery. The spelling out creates escalating comedy and became culturally quotable.

jokes.text: Maggie drives away in car, Homer gets dragged behind making 'Ow!' sounds
jokes.setup: Homer asks baby Maggie if she wants to go to Blockoland
jokes.punchline: Baby somehow drives car away, dragging Homer painfully behind
jokes.explanation: Standard Simpsons slapstick with baby-drives-car absurdity. Well-executed but not particularly original.

jokes.text: Abraham Lincoln animatronic malfunctioning mid-speech
jokes.setup: Lincoln begins formal 'Four score and seven' speech
jokes.punchline: Robot breaks down screaming mid-sentence
jokes.explanation: Solid physical comedy about cheap theme park attractions failing.

jokes.text: Lisa's robotic monotone voice: 'I am a robot. Do what I say' and washing machine variant
jokes.setup: Playing with blocks at theme park
jokes.punchline: Perfectly monotone robot impression followed by washing machine
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Lisa's dry wit and intelligence.

jokes.text: Homer builds 'healthy apple' out of ham cubes
jokes.setup: Homer proudly shows off his healthy creation
jokes.punchline: It's made entirely of ham but he calls it an apple
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - self-deceptive rationalization. Great character integration.

jokes.text: Guest screaming about leeches in block water
jokes.setup: Problems with the block water attraction
jokes.punchline: Customer emerges screaming about leeches
jokes.explanation: Basic escalation of theme park problems. Functional but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Homer's defeatist life advice: 'Welcome to real life, Lisa. You can't fight city hall'
jokes.setup: Lisa's missing piece prompts parental wisdom
jokes.punchline: Homer delivers crushing nihilistic advice to a child
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer parenting - inappropriately adult cynicism delivered to a child. Sets up his character arc.

jokes.text: Homer admits he tells his children this defeatist message constantly
jokes.punchline: He admits he tells them this regularly, even at bedtime
jokes.explanation: Great escalation showing Homer's terrible parenting is systematic, not accidental.

jokes.text: Homer ironically announces 'The Simpsons are going to Blockoland!' as they leave
jokes.setup: Family is leaving the terrible theme park
jokes.punchline: Homer cheerfully announces they're going there
jokes.explanation: Good ironic timing and Homer's obliviousness to context.

jokes.text: Gift shop clerk's philosophy: 'You can't fight the souvenir industry. We're too powerful'
jokes.punchline: Clerk frames gift shops as an unstoppable force
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of corporate power to absurd proportions. Great economy.

jokes.text: Homer threatens violence then gives the clerk a music box souvenir
jokes.setup: Homer winds up for violence: 'I'm gonna give you a souvenir of my own!'
jokes.punchline: He literally gives him a gentle music box gift
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection and character growth moment. Excellent structure and economy.

jokes.text: Homer accidentally helps bullies beat up their victim's shirt fire
jokes.setup: Homer tries to help by fighting off bullies
jokes.punchline: They were actually putting out the victim's fire
jokes.explanation: Great ironic reversal. Homer's good intentions backfire perfectly.

jokes.text: Victim calls Homer an idiot for stopping fire assistance, Homer: 'My heart was in the right place, jerk!'
jokes.punchline: Self-righteous response despite being completely wrong
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer defense mechanism - can't admit fault. Great character integration.

jokes.text: Homer's negotiation: 'Please, ugly is such a smelly word'
jokes.punchline: Homer's nonsensical word association
jokes.explanation: Brilliant nonsensical logic. Perfect Homer brain malfunction with great economy.

jokes.text: Homer's sales pitch: 'Bart has inner beauty, like you'd find in a rodent'
jokes.setup: Homer tries to highlight Bart's positive qualities
jokes.punchline: Compares him to a rodent
jokes.explanation: Excellent backhanded compliment. Homer's terrible advocacy skills perfectly displayed.

jokes.text: Homer's brutal honesty to the girl about her flaws: braces, dresses like a kid, aging
jokes.setup: Homer pivots to attacking the girl instead
jokes.punchline: Devastating personal inventory of her shortcomings
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation and cringe comedy. Homer's negotiation tactics are hilariously inappropriate.

jokes.text: Homer threatens to unscrew jar lids as intimidation tactic
jokes.punchline: Menacing jar lid unscrewing as a threat
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd escalation. Mundane action presented as serious threat.

jokes.text: Homer's accidental business consulting about profit margins being 'razor thin'
jokes.punchline: Accidentally becomes helpful business consultant
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection from threat to helpful advice. Subverts expectation well.

jokes.text: Homer's humble response trailing off: 'The satisfaction of helping another human being is all the thanks l- et cetera'
jokes.setup: Marge thanks Homer for his help
jokes.punchline: Homer cuts off his own humble speech with 'et cetera'
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy. Homer's laziness cutting off his own noble speech is excellent character work.

jokes.text: Homer's specific boast about giving wrong directions: 'that's the kind of guy I am this week'
jokes.punchline: Admits he gave wrong directions but frames it as heroic
jokes.explanation: Great character insight. Homer's self-awareness about his temporary personality change.

jokes.text: Homer gave directions 'even though I didn't know the way, 'cause that's the kind of guy I am this week'
jokes.punchline: Admits he gave false directions and frames his helpfulness as temporary phase
jokes.explanation: 'This week' perfectly captures Homer's fleeting commitments. High quotability for the phrase structure.

jokes.text: Homer's geographic confusion about baseball office locations
jokes.setup: Homer looking for the executive office
jokes.punchline: Series of wrong building identifications
jokes.explanation: Basic Homer confusion. Gets some laughs but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Executive's panicked response to Homer seeing wrong door and room contents
jokes.setup: Homer about to enter wrong door
jokes.punchline: Executive's desperate 'No, no! Not that door!'
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the discovery. Executive's panic sells the secret well.

jokes.text: Homer discovers 'Albuquerque Isotopes' merchandise and evidence
jokes.punchline: Room full of evidence about team relocation
jokes.explanation: Good plot revelation. Sets up the main conflict effectively.

jokes.text: Executive's terrible lying: 'It's not anything. It has no purpose.'
jokes.setup: Homer asks about the suspicious merchandise
jokes.punchline: Obviously false denials about the evidence
jokes.explanation: Basic incompetent lying. Functional but not particularly inspired.

jokes.text: Homer's easy bribery: 'Well, okay. But you can't silence Homer Simpson!'
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately accepts while declaring he won't be silenced
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer contradiction. Declares integrity while being easily bought.

jokes.text: Duffman's entrance and corporate beer promotion
jokes.punchline: Over-the-top corporate mascot entrance with 'Oh yeah!'
jokes.explanation: Good character introduction. Duffman's corporate enthusiasm is well-established.

jokes.text: Homer's confused awakening and swing relationship: 'But I love the swing'
jokes.setup: Marge tells Homer he was yelling at the swing
jokes.punchline: Homer's confused affection for the swing
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist detail. Homer's relationship with inanimate objects is funny.

jokes.text: 'Joel Duffman, The Newsly Times' - obvious fake journalist identity
jokes.punchline: Corporate mascot poses as journalist with transparent fake name and publication
jokes.explanation: Good corporate corruption satire. 'The Newsly Times' is a perfect fake newspaper name.

jokes.text: Fake footage of Homer with 'pants on fire' as evidence he's a liar
jokes.setup: Mr. Duff claims Homer is a liar
jokes.punchline: Literal interpretation of 'pants on fire' idiom
jokes.explanation: Brilliant literal interpretation of idiom. Great visual gag and wordplay.

jokes.text: Homer's philosophical distinction about when he minds being called a liar
jokes.setup: Homer upset about being called a liar
jokes.punchline: Detailed breakdown of when lying accusations are acceptable to him
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment. Homer's twisted logic about lying ethics is perfectly crafted.

jokes.text: Marge's observation: 'Homie, you couldn't keep up a hunger strike. You eat while you brush your teeth.'
jokes.punchline: Marge's specific example of Homer's eating habits
jokes.explanation: Perfect character observation. Specific detail about eating while brushing teeth is brilliantly absurd.

jokes.text: Homer eating sausage while denying he can't stop eating
jokes.setup: Homer claims he can stop eating
jokes.punchline: Currently eating a huge sausage
jokes.explanation: Classic visual contradiction. Homer's actions undermining his words perfectly.

jokes.text: Homer's immediate hunger strike breakdown with crying and eating
jokes.setup: Homer declares hunger strike starting 'right now'
jokes.punchline: Immediate collapse and resumed eating
jokes.explanation: Predictable but well-executed. Homer's immediate failure is character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Lisa's gift of Mike Farrell's core beliefs book and Homer's reaction to hating Wayne Rogers
jokes.punchline: Homer's focus on celebrity feuds instead of principles
jokes.explanation: Good pop culture reference. Homer missing the point for celebrity gossip is character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Kid calling father 'Weekend Dad' and being corrected
jokes.setup: Child thanking father for ball game
jokes.punchline: Awkward family dynamic revealed
jokes.explanation: Good background character moment. Brief but effective social observation.

jokes.text: Seymour and Edna's food flirtation interrupted by Homer's desperation
jokes.punchline: Homer's starving interruption and God temptation resistance
jokes.explanation: Good hunger strike torture. Juxtaposition of romance and starvation works well.

jokes.text: Homer's hunger strike song with Jesus comparison disclaimer
jokes.punchline: Specific disclaimer about not being sacrilegious
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-commentary. Homer's self-awareness about religious sensitivity is funny.

jokes.text: Dr. Hibbert's explanation: 'That's his stomach eating itself'
jokes.setup: Child asking about Homer's stomach sounds
jokes.punchline: Casually horrifying medical explanation
jokes.explanation: Good dark comedy. Hibbert's casual delivery of horrifying fact is character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Duffman's third-person corporate speak: 'Duffman is thrusting in the direction of the problem'
jokes.punchline: Bizarrely sexual corporate assessment
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Duffman line. Sexual innuendo in corporate speak is perfectly character-driven.

jokes.text: Reference to 'busty woman who ran out on the field and farted at the ballplayers'
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific and inappropriate incident cited as positive precedent
jokes.explanation: Absurd sports incident that feels believable in the Simpsons universe. Specific enough to be memorable.

jokes.text: Stadium announcer's lie: 'He's on a hunger strike till the 'Topes win the pennant!'
jokes.punchline: Completely false motivation for hunger strike
jokes.explanation: Good corporate manipulation. False narrative is believable and well-executed.

jokes.text: Homer's weak correction being misheard as 'Go, 'Topes!'
jokes.setup: Homer tries to correct the false narrative
jokes.punchline: His weakness makes truth inaudible
jokes.explanation: Good irony. Homer's physical state preventing truth from being heard.

jokes.text: Excessive food vendor announcements torturing starving Homer
jokes.punchline: Increasingly elaborate food descriptions
jokes.explanation: Standard hunger torture sequence. Works but predictable.

jokes.text: Homer's body deterioration: 'I'm down to a B cup!'
jokes.punchline: Using bra sizes to measure his body
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Homer logic. Using bra sizes for weight measurement is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: Baseball executives' casual attitude: 'Death is a part of baseball. Oh, yeah. The main part.'
jokes.punchline: Executives treating death as normal baseball element
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor. Corporate callousness perfectly captured with great escalation.

jokes.text: Using sprinklers to make unconscious Homer appear to move
jokes.punchline: Artificial movement from water pressure
jokes.explanation: Good dark visual gag. Corporate deception taken to extreme lengths.

jokes.text: Cesar Chavez appearing as Cesar Romero due to Homer's ignorance
jokes.punchline: Homer's brain substitutes familiar Cesar
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character-driven comedy. Homer's ignorance creating wrong historical figure is perfect.

jokes.text: Replacement attraction: 'Paint-drinking Pete!'
jokes.punchline: Even more dangerous replacement act
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of dangerous promotions. Paint-drinking is appropriately absurd.

jokes.text: Homer's weakened speech: 'Me so hungy'
jokes.punchline: Broken English from starvation
jokes.explanation: Basic hunger effect. Gets sympathy laughs but not particularly creative.

jokes.text: Duffman's moral awakening: 'New feelings brewing in Duffman! What would Jesus do?'
jokes.punchline: Corporate mascot having spiritual crisis
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character development. Corporate mascot finding conscience is perfectly executed.

jokes.text: Homer's triumphant line: 'The truth never tasted so good!'
jokes.setup: Homer finally eating after hunger strike victory
jokes.punchline: Connecting truth and taste satisfaction
jokes.explanation: Good victory line. Connects the moral victory with physical satisfaction perfectly.

jokes.text: Albuquerque mayor's confusion about football vs baseball: 'They'll play what I tell them to play'
jokes.setup: Aide correcting mayor about Cowboys being football
jokes.punchline: Mayor's authoritarian dismissal of sports distinctions
jokes.explanation: Good closing character moment. Mayor's oblivious authority is well-crafted.

tmdb_overview: Homer becomes a Good Samaritan after discovering the simple joys of helping people in need–which is put to the test when he goes on a hunger strike after the owner of the Springfield Isotopes attempts to discredit him when Homer stumbles on his plot to discreetly move the team to Albuquerque, New Mexico.
